headline: JD.com launches Joybuy in Europe, targeting Amazon
summary: The launch comes as Chinese retailers and brands have been expanding abroad in the U.S. and Europe, looking for new growth drivers away ‌from the cut-throat competition and ⁠weak consumer demand they face at home. Its ⁠platform will also feature dedicated brand stores including L'Oreal, Braun, DeLonghi, BRITA, and Bodum.

headline: JD.com’s European Push Pits Chinese Retailer Against Amazon
summary: The online retailer founded by billionaire Richard Liu aims to draw customers to its Joybuy.com with a promise of fast delivery of products, ranging from Chinese food, fridges and televisions to toys and cosmetics.  Using its own website represents a strategic shift for JD.com in the UK, where it had considered a takeover of electronics retailer Currys Plc and was in talks to buy general merchandiser Argos from J Sainsbury Plc before the deal fell apart.

content.title: JD.com’s European Push Pits Chinese Retailer Against Amazon
content.summary: The online retailer founded by billionaire Richard Liu aims to draw customers to its Joybuy.com with a promise of fast delivery of products, ranging from Chinese food, fridges and televisions to toys and cosmetics.  Using its own website represents a strategic shift for JD.com in the UK, where it had considered a takeover of electronics retailer Currys Plc and was in talks to buy general merchandiser Argos from J Sainsbury Plc before the deal fell apart.

headline: Foxconn's profit lags estimates as it forecasts strong revenue growth
summary: Taiwan's Foxconn, the world's largest contract electronics maker, said on Monday it expected strong revenue growth in the first quarter and the whole of this year, even as it ‌posted a fall of 2% in quarterly profit, lagging estimates.  The technology giant, Nvidia's biggest server maker and Apple's top iPhone assembler, ‌blamed a rise in its tax rate for the drop, which came despite robust global demand for AI products and a jump of 22% in fourth-quarter revenue.  Foxconn's guidance for ​both first quarter and full year revenue are for "strong growth", the highest outlook it can give, as the company does not provide a numeric outlook.

content.title: Foxconn's profit lags estimates as it forecasts strong revenue growth
content.summary: Taiwan's Foxconn, the world's largest contract electronics maker, said on Monday it expected strong revenue growth in the first quarter and the whole of this year, even as it ‌posted a fall of 2% in quarterly profit, lagging estimates.  The technology giant, Nvidia's biggest server maker and Apple's top iPhone assembler, ‌blamed a rise in its tax rate for the drop, which came despite robust global demand for AI products and a jump of 22% in fourth-quarter revenue.  Foxconn's guidance for ​both first quarter and full year revenue are for "strong growth", the highest outlook it can give, as the company does not provide a numeric outlook.

headline: Beyond Market Price: Uncovering Amazon.com Inc's Intrinsic Value
summary: Instead of using future free cash flow as in the traditional DCF model, the GuruFocus DCF calculator uses EPS without NRI as the default for the DCF model based on research that shows that historically stock prices have been more correlated with earnings than free cash flow. Warning! GuruFocus has detected 2 Warning Sign with AMZN.

content.title: Beyond Market Price: Uncovering Amazon.com Inc's Intrinsic Value
content.summary: Instead of using future free cash flow as in the traditional DCF model, the GuruFocus DCF calculator uses EPS without NRI as the default for the DCF model based on research that shows that historically stock prices have been more correlated with earnings than free cash flow. Warning! GuruFocus has detected 2 Warning Sign with AMZN.

headline: Chase Freedom® Announces Q2 2026 5% Cash Back Categories: Amazon, Whole Foods Market, Chase Travel℠ and Feeding America®
summary: WILMINGTON, Del., March 16, 2026--Today, Chase announced that Freedom and Freedom Flex cardmembers can earn 5% cash back on Amazon, Whole Foods Market and Chase Travel purchases, plus donations to Feeding America from April 1 through June 30, 2026. The new categories are versatile and practical for day-to-day spending, so cardmembers can start planning everything from next week’s grocery haul to springtime vacations.

content.title: Chase Freedom® Announces Q2 2026 5% Cash Back Categories: Amazon, Whole Foods Market, Chase Travel℠ and Feeding America®
content.summary: WILMINGTON, Del., March 16, 2026--Today, Chase announced that Freedom and Freedom Flex cardmembers can earn 5% cash back on Amazon, Whole Foods Market and Chase Travel purchases, plus donations to Feeding America from April 1 through June 30, 2026. The new categories are versatile and practical for day-to-day spending, so cardmembers can start planning everything from next week’s grocery haul to springtime vacations.

headline: Meta to Spend Up to $27 Billion on Nebius AI Infrastructure
summary: Nebius, a so-called neocloud that operates data centers and has a strategic partnership with Nvidia Corp., will provide Meta $12 billion of dedicated capacity starting in early 2027, the Dutch company said in a statement Monday.  Meta also committed to buying as much as $15 billion in additional capacity that the cloud provider is building for third-party customers.

content.title: Meta to Spend Up to $27 Billion on Nebius AI Infrastructure
content.summary: Nebius, a so-called neocloud that operates data centers and has a strategic partnership with Nvidia Corp., will provide Meta $12 billion of dedicated capacity starting in early 2027, the Dutch company said in a statement Monday.  Meta also committed to buying as much as $15 billion in additional capacity that the cloud provider is building for third-party customers.

headline: JD.com Launches Joybuy in UK and Europe, Challenging Amazon With Same-Day Delivery
summary: JD.com (NASDAQ:JD) is preparing to push deeper into Europe, launching its Joybuy platform in the UK and several other European markets as the Chinese e-commerce group looks beyond its home turf.  The move comes as competition inside China's online retail sector intensifies, with companies battling for market share and engaging in heavy discounting that has pressured margins across the industry.  In London and selected cities across the UK, Germany, France, Belgium, Luxembourg and the Netherlands, Joybuy is promoting a double 11 delivery promiseorders placed before 11 a.m. could arrive by 11 p.m. the same day.

content.title: JD.com Launches Joybuy in UK and Europe, Challenging Amazon With Same-Day Delivery
content.summary: JD.com (NASDAQ:JD) is preparing to push deeper into Europe, launching its Joybuy platform in the UK and several other European markets as the Chinese e-commerce group looks beyond its home turf.  The move comes as competition inside China's online retail sector intensifies, with companies battling for market share and engaging in heavy discounting that has pressured margins across the industry.  In London and selected cities across the UK, Germany, France, Belgium, Luxembourg and the Netherlands, Joybuy is promoting a double 11 delivery promiseorders placed before 11 a.m. could arrive by 11 p.m. the same day.
